entries.proves: At least one parallel AARP signature verified under a trusted key over the canonical signed assertion payload.
A verifying signature's key id is bound by a verifier-side trust entry to the asserted mediator identity, role, and trust domain when scoped.
does_not_prove: that the mediator made a correct decision
that all traffic was forced through the mediator
that the key was never compromised

entries.proves: The signed assertion carries a well-formed Rung-1 chain link with a sequence number and prior payload hash.
does_not_prove: that the whole stream was verified as contiguous

entries.proves: The X.509-SVID leaf chain validated offline against the pinned bundle at action time and the SPIFFE ID was permitted.
does_not_prove: that the workload's egress was non-bypassable

entries.proves: The SVID leaf key signed the receipt and assertion binding, proving possession by the signing workload identity.
does_not_prove: that local side effects were mediated

entries.proves: The X.509-SVID was valid at the action time under the pinned historical bundle.
does_not_prove: that the assertion was published promptly
that the key remained uncompromised later
that the chain was witnessed externally

entries.proves: Reserved for the claim that the decision policy hash is bound into the signed assertion.
does_not_prove: that the policy was enforced across unmediated traffic
that every evaluator used the same policy

entries.proves: Reserved for stream-level verification that an issuer chain is contiguous for the checked range.
does_not_prove: absence of records before or after the checked range

entries.proves: Reserved for verification that an external witness signed the checkpoint being checked.
does_not_prove: that all receipts were submitted to the witness
that the witness showed the same view to every verifier
that the checkpoint covers a specific action

entries.proves: Reserved for verification that the receipt-chain root matches the externally witnessed checkpoint root.
does_not_prove: that omitted receipts do not exist
that the witness is globally consistent
that every action stream was submitted

entries.proves: Reserved for verification that a witnessed checkpoint covers the asserted issuer chain sequence range.
does_not_prove: that all receipts before the range were submitted
that actions outside the range were mediated

entries.proves: Reserved for verification that the external witness observation time for a checkpoint was recorded and validated.
does_not_prove: wall-clock correctness beyond the witness evidence
that the receipt was emitted at that time

entries.proves: Reserved for observing that the witness-silence interval after the last witnessed checkpoint stayed within the configured bound, assuming process, clock, witness path, and deployment boundary were operating.
does_not_prove: that every receipt was submitted to the witness
that the deployment boundary was non-bypassable

entries.proves: Reserved for attested observation that a Kubernetes namespace egress policy restricts the workload to the mediator path.
does_not_prove: that a cluster administrator cannot bypass it
that workloads outside the observed scope are covered

entries.proves: Reserved for attested observation that the workload pod spec contains the expected Pipelock proxy injection.
does_not_prove: that the running pod state still matches after snapshot or mutation
that node root cannot bypass the proxy
that hosted SaaS side actions were mediated

entries.proves: Reserved for attested observation that the Kubernetes workload identity is bound to the mediator policy under review.
does_not_prove: that the workload identity key was never compromised
that direct node-level egress is impossible

entries.proves: Reserved for attested observation that the Kubernetes admission policy hash is bound to the appraisal evidence.
does_not_prove: that admission policy is semantically correct
that cluster administrators cannot bypass admission

entries.proves: Reserved for attested observation that a Linux process-owner egress rule was present for the mediated workload.
does_not_prove: that root cannot bypass the rule
that all descendant processes are covered

entries.proves: The appraisal explicitly does not assert that no bypass path existed.
The appraisal explicitly does not assert that the action was safe.
The appraisal explicitly does not assert that every tool or action surface was discovered.
The appraisal explicitly does not assert complete mediation.
The appraisal explicitly does not assert that delegated actions were mediated.
The appraisal explicitly does not assert that the policy or control was effective.
The appraisal explicitly does not assert that hosted SaaS side actions were mediated.
The appraisal explicitly does not assert that the action matched user intent.
The appraisal explicitly does not assert that any signing key was never compromised.
The appraisal explicitly does not assert that local side effects were mediated.
The appraisal explicitly does not assert that the policy was correct.
The appraisal explicitly does not assert semantic equivalence after any modifying step.
The appraisal explicitly states that a verified workload identity does not prove network non-bypass.
The appraisal explicitly states that a verified workload identity does not prove deployment enforcement.
Reserved limitation stating that witness evidence does not assert all receipts were submitted to the witness.
Reserved limitation stating that witness evidence does not assert absence of omissions before the witnessed checkpoint.
Reserved limitation stating that witness evidence without gossip does not assert absence of split view.
Reserved limitation stating that witness evidence does not assert action-stream completeness.
Reserved limitation stating that deployment evidence does not assert a cluster administrator cannot bypass controls.
Reserved limitation stating that deployment evidence does not assert node root cannot bypass controls.
Reserved limitation stating that deployment evidence does not assert CNI enforcement correctness.
Reserved limitation stating that deployment evidence does not assert hosted SaaS side actions were mediated.
Reserved limitation stating that deployment evidence does not assert runtime state stayed unchanged after snapshot or restore.
Reserved limitation stating that deployment evidence does not assert all namespaces or workloads are covered.
The appraisal warns that a valid signature is being reported without a transparency-axis proof.
The appraisal warns that a bound SVID identity is being reported without a deployment-axis non-bypass proof.
The appraisal warns that a single signed chain link is being reported without stream-level contiguous-chain verification.
